headline: 26 contracts catalogued across 5 chains; 1 bridge system, 10 earn/token products, 4 treasury contracts, 11 supporting contracts.
short_headline: 26 contracts across 5 chains; bridge + earn system

findings.text: Block explorer name-tag search: No Aster Bridge-specific name tags found on Etherscan, Arbitrum Scan, or Optimism; protocol primarily operates as perpetual DEX with integrated Aster Chain L1.

Official deployments doc: https://docs.asterdex.com/overview/smart-contracts.md lists Aster Treasury, Aster Earn (asBTC, asUSDF, asBNB, asCAKE), and USDF Stablecoin contracts across BNB Chain, Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Solana.

Audit PDFs: 7 audits retrieved: Salus Security (AsterVault Sep 2024), Salus Security (asBNB Dec 2024), Salus Security (asCAKE Dec 2024), PeckShield (asBNB, USDF 2024), Halborn (USDF & asUSDF 2024), plus references to Trail of Bits, Halborn, and Zellic audits for Aster Chain bridge.

GitHub deployment artifacts: asterdex organization identified at https://github.com/asterdex with 8 repositories (API docs, API demo, SDK, MCP, skills hub, etc.); specific smart contract repo and broadcast artifacts not yet accessed.

Multi-chain enumeration: Aster operates on 5 chains per pinned context (Binance/BNB, Ethereum, Arbitrum, Solana, Scroll); contract addresses found for BNB Chain, Ethereum, Arbitrum, Solana; Scroll addresses not yet located.
Factory-discovered children: No factory pattern enumeration attempted; Aster Earn products appear to be discrete minting contracts per asset (asBTC, asUSDF, asBNB, asCAKE), not dynamically generated.

Role taxonomy: Treasury and earning contracts identified with roles: treasury (fund management), oracle (Aster Chain uses weighted median from 14 major exchanges), validator (PoSA-based, 3-of-4 for deposits, 2-of-3 for withdrawals), minting (token issuance).
Ratchet output integrity: All addresses traced to official docs; no snippet-only sightings. All admin/control addresses require on-chain verification in next phase.

rationale.verdict: Discovery identified 26 contracts deployed across Binance, Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Solana; Aster Chain is the core L1 with integrated bridge system supporting cross-chain deposits and withdrawals. Audits by Salus Security, PeckShield, and Halborn cover earn products and vault. Bridge contracts documented as forthcoming public disclosure. Scroll deployment addresses not yet located—recorded in unknowns.

evidence.shows: Official Aster smart contract addresses: Aster Treasury on BNB Chain (0x128463A60784c4D3f46c23Af3f65Ed859Ba87974), Ethereum (0x604DD02d620633Ae427888d41bfd15e38483736E), Solana (EhUtRgu9iEbZXXRpEvDj6n1wnQRjMi2SERDo3c6bmN2c), Arbitrum (0x9E36CB86a159d479cEd94Fa05036f235Ac40E1d5). Aster Earn: asBTC Token 0x184b72289c0992BDf96751354680985a7C4825d6, asBTC Minting 0x8a3C77E6c6A488d26CD44F403b95e44675f46e6A, asUSDF Token 0x917AF46B3C3c6e1Bb7286B9F59637Fb7C65851Fb, asUSDF Minting 0xdB57a53C428a9faFcbFefFB6dd80d0f427543695, asBNB Token 0x77734e70b6E88b4d82fE632a168EDf6e700912b6, asBNB Minting 0x2F31ab8950c50080E77999fa456372f276952fD8, asCAKE Token 0x9817F4c9f968a553fF6caEf1a2ef6cF1386F16F7, asCAKE Minting 0x1A81A28482Edd40ff1689CB3D857c3dAdF11D502. USDF Stablecoin: USDF Token 0x5A110fC00474038f6c02E89C707D638602EA44B5, USDF Minting 0xC271fc70dD9E678ac1AB632f797894fe4BE2C345.

Aster Chain is a Layer 1 blockchain with PoSA consensus, providing cross-chain bridge system supporting deposits and withdrawals via 3-of-4 validator verification for deposits and 2-of-3 for withdrawals. Supported networks: BNB Chain, Ethereum, Arbitrum, Solana. Bridge contracts to be publicly disclosed on Smart Contracts page.

Audit reports: AsterVault by Salus (2024-09-13), AsterEarn by unknown firm (2024-09-12), asBNB by Salus (2024-12-11), asBNB by PeckShield, USDF by PeckShield, USDF & asUSDF by Halborn, asCAKE by Salus (2024-12-17). Referenced auditors for Aster Chain: Trail of Bits, Halborn Security, Zellic.
Official asterdex organization on GitHub with 8 repositories including API documentation, API demo, skills hub, broker pro SDK, connector SDK, MCP, and other developer tools.

Salus Security audit of AstherusVault (v2) on EVM-compatible chains; GitHub repo https://github.com/leek-z/astherus-contract; 0 critical, 1 medium, 4 low, 0 informational issues. Date: Sep 13, 2024. Commit: 98606bc.

unknowns: D5: Scroll chain deployment addresses not located despite search across Scrollscan and docs.
D4: GitHub smart contract repository URL not yet confirmed; only API and SDK repos found at asterdex org.
D6-offchain: Factory enumeration skipped; Aster Earn contracts appear static, not factory-generated.
Bridge-audits-offchain: Bridge audits by Trail of Bits, Halborn, Zellic referenced in testnet launch article but audit PDFs not linked in official audit-reports.md page.

protocol_metadata.about: Aster is a decentralized perpetual exchange offering MEV-free trading with up to 1001x leverage. It includes Aster Chain, a Layer 1 blockchain with integrated cross-chain bridge supporting deposits and withdrawals across Ethereum, BNB Chain, Arbitrum, and Solana. The protocol powers multiple earning products (asBTC, asUSDF, asBNB, asCAKE) and a native USDF stablecoin. Audited by Salus Security, PeckShield, and Halborn.
